rawAdvertText:
Head of Clinical Services and Education - job post
The Pharmacist Network
Remote
£60,000 - £65,000 a year - Full-time
Job details
Here’s how the job details align with your profile.

Pay


£60,000 - £65,000 a year


Job type


Full-time

 
Full job description
ABOUT US


The Pharmacist Network Group (TPN Group) is a leading private healthcare organisation supporting Primary Care across the UK. The group brings together The Pharmacist Network (TPN), MORPh, and MORPh Clinical Services (MCS), combining workforce, education, and clinical service delivery to create one of the most connected pharmacy-led platforms in UK primary care.

This role sits primarily within MORPh and MORPh Clinical Services. MORPh delivers high-quality pharmaceutical education through face-to-face events, webinars, masterclasses, and sponsored educational programmes. MORPh Clinical Services works directly with GP practices and Primary Care Networks to deliver medication optimisation, therapy reviews, and wider clinical support services.

We are at an exciting stage of growth and transformation, with continued investment in our infrastructure, systems, and people to support our expanding national footprint. This role offers the opportunity to be at the centre of that journey, contributing to both operational delivery and future development.

THE ROLE

This is a senior clinical leadership role with real breadth. You will own the clinical and educational heart of the business leading our Neighbourhood Education Lead network, building our content library, driving speaker relationships, and shaping the educational programmes we develop and deliver. At the same time, you will have a direct commercial seat at the table working with pharmaceutical partners, contributing to business development, and translating clinical expertise into structured programmes that extend the reach and impact of your work.

You will report directly to the Director of Operations and lead one direct report.

For the right person, this is a rare opportunity to bring clinical credibility into a commercially driven environment and to see the direct impact of your work on a business that is growing fast.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Clinical Leadership and Content

Build and maintain a centralised CPD-accredited content library pre-loaded modules, slides, and interactive activities that form the backbone of our event programme
Create event agendas centrally, ensuring clinical quality, educational integrity, and relevance to our delegate audience
Lead on educational content and blog development recruiting writers, developing briefs, QA, and managing pharma approval
Ensure all educational outputs meet CPD, ABPI, and regulatory standards
Act as the primary contact with the CPD accreditation body managing all submissions and ongoing liaison

Neighbourhood Education Lead Network Leadership

Own the Neighbourhood Education Lead (NEL) relationship at a senior level responsible for recruitment, onboarding, group meetings, governance, and quality assurance across all NEL activity
Provide NELs with the content, tools, and frameworks they need to operate as facilitators and brand ambassadors
Build and maintain strong individual relationships across a network of approximately 40 NELs nationally

Speaker Management and Faculty Development

Identify, vet, and develop a high-quality national speaker faculty
Conduct virtual review meetings with speakers ahead of bookings
Review speaker presentations for clinical neutrality and alignment with event objectives

Stakeholder Engagement

Contribute directly to business development writing masterclass agendas, providing clinical insight for BD conversations, and attending sponsor meetings as the clinical voice
Build and maintain strong working relationships with pharmaceutical partners ensuring alignment between sponsor objectives and educational integrity
Act as the senior clinical contact for toolkit and masterclass projects once live

Programme and Academy Development

Lead the development of a portfolio of training programmes, including:
New to Primary Care
Pharmacy Technicians
Foundation Pharmacists
Early Career Pharmacists
New to Prescribing Pathway
Lead the development of specialist academies including a Leadership Academy, Pharmacy Technicians Academy, and Early Careers Pharmacists Academy

Morph Clinical Services

Provide clinical oversight and leadership across MCS activity supporting GP surgeries with medication optimisation and therapy review programmes
Ensure consistency and quality across MCS delivery
ABOUT YOU

Essential

Clinical background, ideally with some experience in as a primary care pharmacist IP qualification preferred
Strong track record of building and maintaining professional relationships with clinicians, commercial partners, or both
Genuine interest in clinical education and a belief in its value to patient outcomes
Experience of creating clinical content, agendas, or educational programmes
Comfortable working at the intersection of clinical and commercial able to think about how educational expertise can be structured, packaged, and scaled
Desirable

Experience working with or within pharmaceutical education, sponsored events, or medical affairs
Familiarity with CPD accreditation processes and ABPI Code requirements
Experience managing or leading a professional network or faculty
WHAT WE OFFER

£60,000 £65,000 depending on experience
Permanent contract
Remote working with occasional travel to our Newcastle office and events nationally
The opportunity to shape a function that is being built from the ground up
Direct exposure to commercial, operational, and strategic leadership at a growing healthcare group
roleTitle: Head of Clinical Services and Education
location: Remote; occasional travel to Newcastle office and events nationally
shortCompanyReason: Leading private healthcare organisation supporting primary care across the UK.
shortRoleReason: Senior clinical leadership role spanning education, content, speaker networks, and clinical services.
companySummary: TPN Group combines workforce, education, and clinical service delivery across The Pharmacist Network, MORPh, and MORPh Clinical Services.
roleSummary: Lead clinical education, content, speaker and network management, while also providing oversight to clinical services and supporting commercial activity.
headlineAttraction: Rare chance to combine clinical credibility with commercial influence in a fast-growing healthcare group.
rolePurpose: To lead the clinical and educational function, ensuring high-quality programmes and services that can be scaled nationally.
travelRequired: Occasional travel to Newcastle office and events nationally
advertSummary: A senior remote role for a clinically credible pharmacist leader to shape education programmes, manage a national network and speaker faculty, support pharmaceutical partners, and oversee clinical services delivery.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right at the point where I do my best work: bringing structure to complex services, improving how things run, and making sure the output is credible and useful. My background is not a straight pharmacy career, but I’ve spent years leading regulated, service-led operations where quality, stakeholder trust, and measurable delivery all mattered.
whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I want to do next: building repeatable programmes, tightening governance, and helping a service scale without losing quality. I would be comfortable working across education, network management, clinical oversight, and sponsor-facing activity because I’ve spent my career balancing delivery, standards, and stakeholder expectations.

Investment in infrastructure, systems, and people
Opportunity to shape a function from the ground up

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Clinical operations leader who can make education, quality, and delivery work together.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this role because it sits right at the point where I do my best work: bringing structure to complex services, improving how things run, and making sure the output is credible and useful. My background is not a straight pharmacy career, but I’ve spent years leading regulated, service-led operations where quality, stakeholder trust, and measurable delivery all mattered.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: TPN Group stands out to me because it combines workforce, education, and clinical service delivery in one place, and it is clearly investing in the systems and people needed to scale that model nationally. I’m also drawn to the fact that the business is growing fast while keeping clinical and educational work close to the commercial side, which is the kind of environment where I can add value.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role fits the kind of work I want to do next: building repeatable programmes, tightening governance, and helping a service scale without losing quality. I would be comfortable working across education, network management, clinical oversight, and sponsor-facing activity because I’ve spent my career balancing delivery, standards, and stakeholder expectations.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: clinical quality and service improvement in a live healthcare setting
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I can improve patient-facing performance in NHS primary care, not just talk about quality in theory. The scale of the turnaround also shows I can work with live service issues and move outcomes in the right direction.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a headline proof point for healthcare credibility and service improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I improved patient experience in NHS primary care, taking Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Data-led rota and capacity thinking for clinical workforce
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: workforce/network coordination and practical clinical cover planning
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because the role needs someone who can think clearly about how people, cover, and service demand fit together. Even though the setting was different, the underlying challenge was the same: making capacity more workable and less reactive.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for network, onboarding, or service planning discussions.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve worked on structured rota and capacity thinking where cover and demand needed to be managed properly.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: stakeholder management, governance, and delivery in a regulated environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I’m used to handling complex, business-critical work while keeping standards intact. That matters here because the role sits across education, commercial activity, and regulated outputs.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for governance, reporting, and managing competing priorities.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led complex, high-stakes work in a regulated environment while keeping service standards in place.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My reading of this role is that it needs someone who can turn clinical credibility into something structured, scalable, and commercially usable. It also needs calm judgement: someone who can hold quality, manage relationships, and keep multiple moving parts aligned without losing control of the detail.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve improved a live healthcare service by tightening follow-through and lifting patient experience scores significantly.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I understand how quality is felt by end users, not just written into a process.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in environments where complex work had to be organised, prioritised, and delivered to standard.
experienceMappings.relevance: That translates well to building repeatable education and service frameworks.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked through rota logic, cover constraints, and capacity thinking in a primary care setting.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is relevant to managing a network, onboarding people, and keeping delivery workable.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led work in a high-security, regulated environment where standards and deadlines both mattered.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role needs someone comfortable with QA, approvals, and controlled output.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations through a growth phase where margin, resilience, and delivery discipline all mattered together.
experienceMappings.relevance: That gives me a practical feel for balancing standards with business needs.

focusAreasToBring.title: Clinical content with control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on building content that is organised, reusable, and easy to quality-check, so the education offer can scale without becoming inconsistent.

focusAreasToBring.title: Network and speaker discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would bring a structured approach to relationships, onboarding, and review so the NEL network and speaker faculty stay reliable and aligned.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial and clinical balance
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would be comfortable working with sponsors and partners while protecting educational integrity, because I understand that both sides need to hold.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I’m a strong fit where the job needs structure, judgement, and delivery discipline around a service that has to work in the real world. I bring healthcare experience, regulated-environment experience, and a track record of improving performance without losing sight of the people using the service.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first 6-12 months helping make the education and clinical offer more repeatable, better governed, and easier to scale. I’d also expect to spend time strengthening the working rhythm across the network, speaker activity, and sponsor-facing work so the function can grow without losing quality.
companyHighlights: TPN Group combines workforce, education, and clinical service delivery across The Pharmacist Network, MORPh, and MORPh Clinical Services.
The business is investing in infrastructure, systems, and people while expanding its national footprint.
The role sits close to both operational delivery and future development.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where there is a clear need to build, improve, and keep standards tight while things are moving. The mix of growth, direct leadership access, and practical delivery feels aligned with how I like to work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the current content, network, speaker, and clinical service arrangements, understand where the pressure points are, and identify what already works well enough to keep.
I would focus on how content is governed, how relationships are managed, and where simple controls or templates could make delivery more consistent and easier to scale.
I would aim to have clearer rhythms around quality, approvals, and stakeholder touchpoints, while contributing to the next wave of education and service development.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to helping TPN Group grow its education and clinical offer without losing control of quality.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live NHS primary care service from 2.09 to 4.51 on Google reviews.
I’ve worked with structured capacity and rota thinking in primary care.
I’ve led regulated, business-critical work where standards and delivery both mattered.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct pharmacist, IP, CPD accreditation, or ABPI experience because those are not evidenced in the CV or shortlist provided.
The evidence bank is strongest on operations, healthcare service improvement, and regulated delivery, so I have used those to support fit rather than inventing pharmacy-specific claims.
No company-specific facts beyond the advert were added.

examples.title: “Data-led rota and capacity thinking for clinical workforce”
